changesProposed.name: Astera Labs

current.name: Astera Labs
current.founder: Jitendra Mohan, Sanjay Gajendra, Casey Morrison

verified.name: Astera Labs
verified.description: American fabless semiconductor manufacturer specializing in high-speed connectivity solutions for data centers and AI infrastructure. The company designs PCIe, CXL, Ethernet, and other semiconductor-based solutions to address data, memory, and networking bottlenecks.
verified.notes: Company went public on NASDAQ under ticker ALAB in March 2024. Wikipedia indicates IPO raised $713M at $5.5B valuation. Headquarters relocated to San Jose in June 2025, tripling in size.

changesProposed.name: Cape

verified.name: Cape

changesProposed.name: D-Orbit

verified.name: D-Orbit
verified.description: Private Italian aerospace company providing space logistics and orbital transportation services, including last-mile delivery of satellites using their ION Satellite Carrier orbital transfer vehicle.
verified.notes: Most recent funding round is Series C completed September 27, 2024 for €150 million (US$166 million). Wikipedia notes a planned 2022 SPAC merger with $1.4bn valuation was cancelled. Company has subsidiaries in Portugal, UK, and USA (D-Orbit USA joint venture established July 2024).

changesProposed.name: SpaceX

verified.name: SpaceX
verified.description: American aerospace and AI company founded in 2002 that develops rockets, spacecraft, and satellite constellations. SpaceX conducts more orbital launches annually than any other launch provider and operates the Starlink satellite internet constellation.
verified.notes: Source [1] indicates SpaceX is expected to have an IPO in 2026, but as of the sources provided, it remains a private company. Sources [2], [3], and [4] are news articles discussing electricity, Tesla roadster delays, and Google compute deal respectively—none contain verifiable company facts about SpaceX itself.

changesProposed.name: Starcloud

current.name: Starcloud
current.founder: Philip Johnston, Ezra Feilden, Adi Oltean

verified.name: Starcloud
verified.description: A United States-based company that designs, builds, and deploys data centers in space using proprietary technology. In November 2025, the company launched Starcloud-1 equipped with an Nvidia H100 GPU.
verified.notes: Company was originally founded as 'Lumen Orbit' in El Segundo, California in January 2024 and rebranded to Starcloud in March 2025 following a legal challenge from Lumen Technologies. Series A funding announced March 30, 2026. Fastest unicorn in Y Combinator history (17 months from demo day).
